entries.title: HOMILIES ON THE PSALMS 25:40 on psalms 14:6
entries.excerpt: Like the general over the soldiers and the king over the senate, God is over the generation of the righteous.… If God is in the midst of two or three, how much more is he found in the generation of the righteous where there is a battle-free life, a lack of sadness, rest and inheritance and a kingdom without end.

entries.title: Exposition on Psalm 14 on psalms 14:6
entries.excerpt: "For God is in the just generation." It refers to what went before, so that the sense is, "shall not all they that work iniquity know that the Lord is in the just generation;"  that is, He is not in them who love the world. For it is unjust to leave the Maker of the worlds, and "serve the creature more than the Creator." [Romans 1:25] You have shamed the counsel of the poor, for the Lord is his hope [Psalm 14:6]: that is, you have despised the humble coming of the Son of God, because ye saw not in Him the pomp of the world: that they, whom he was calling, should put their hope in God alone, not in the things that pass away.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON PSALMS 14:6 on psalms 14:6
entries.excerpt: These words that are now put forth can be understood in my opinion in light of the Jews trying to upset the plan of the Savior, because they thought that he was lacking power. They did not consider carefully the treasure of his divinity. So they always contradicted his teaching, and the power of his miracles they misrepresented as not from divine power but as having been worked through some other source.

entries.title: Exposition on the Psalms of David on psalms 14:6
entries.excerpt: And he shows this by a sign: because "you have confounded the counsel of the poor man," that is, you have considered it contemptible and have blasphemed it as far as you were able: and this is the counsel of the poor man, that he may know that the Lord is his hope: Ps. 21: "He hoped in the Lord; let him deliver him, let him save him, since he desires him" (Mt. 27). He who says, "If you wish to be perfect," etc. (Mt. 19). This counsel the rich always despise: Prov. 1: "You have despised all my counsel, and have neglected my reproofs." And whence? "Because the Lord is his hope": because they have nothing in the world from which they may hope except in God, who is the hope of the saints.
